devnet_status.highlight: DevNet 5 spec merged: single proof posted per block on-chain
DevNets fail with >2 subnets; P95 aggregation is 4s vs 0.8s slot interval
DevNet 4/5 branch is 3x slower than main branch due to conservative XMSS parameters
8-core aggregator hardware identified as insufficient; 16-core minimum recommended

technical_blockers.highlight: Main XMSS branch is faster but uses unproven security parameters; decision deferred
Block production (not just aggregation) suspected as root cause for >2 subnet failures
Multi-subnet topology may require an additional ~1–1.2s aggregation interval

client_updates.highlight: Ethlambda has working EL integration via engine API; demo shown running with ethrex, Nethermind, Geth, Erigon, Nimbus, Besu
Ethereum-package updated to support lean clients (lambdaclass/ethereum-package PR #19)

research_and_spec.highlight: Plonky3 optimization PRs merged; full benchmarking suite for WEER and new Monolith arithmetization added
Lean spec stabilization underway; discv5 removed; plan to solicit feedback from beacon client teams and EF
Thomas presenting post-quantum consensus for Ethereum at a Zurich research conference this week

action_items.action: Zeam to upstream aggregate coverage and proposer aggregation metrics to lean spec by tomorrow
Ethlambda to PR block-building improvements to lean spec for cross-team benefit
Determine minimum hardware spec for aggregators (target: next call)

decisions.decision: Spec freeze: no major new features added while teams stabilize DevNets
Keep conservative XMSS parameters for now; revisit main branch only if performance is an absolute blocker
Stabilization target: finality with 1K validators across clients before advancing the spec

targets.target: Tomorrow: Zeam to open PRs for aggregate coverage and proposer aggregation metrics
1–2 months: lean spec ready for broader review by beacon client teams and EF
